diagram.image_prompt: A clean, colorful community centre summer program schedule poster titled 'Maplewood Community Centre — Summer Programs'. Display as a 4-column table with columns: Program, Day & Time, Age Group, Cost. Rows: (1) Beginner Swim — Mon/Wed 10:00-11:00 AM — Ages 6-9 — $80; (2) Youth Pottery — Tue/Thu 4:00-5:30 PM — Ages 10-14 — $120; (3) Senior Yoga — Mon/Wed/Fri 9:00-10:00 AM — Ages 60+ — $60; (4) Family Hiking Club — Saturdays 8:00 AM-12:00 PM — All Ages — $40 per family; (5) Teen Coding Bootcamp — Mon-Fri 1:00-3:00 PM — Ages 13-17 — $200; (6) Toddler Music — Friday 10:30-11:15 AM — Ages 2-4 — $50. Include a footer note: 'Registration closes June 15. Members get 20% off. Drop-in fee: $10 per session where space allows.' Use a friendly summer palette with simple icons next to each program.
diagram.text_description:
Maplewood Community Centre — Summer Programs:
1. Beginner Swim | Mon & Wed 10:00-11:00 AM | Ages 6-9 | $80
2. Youth Pottery | Tue & Thu 4:00-5:30 PM | Ages 10-14 | $120
3. Senior Yoga | Mon/Wed/Fri 9:00-10:00 AM | Ages 60+ | $60
4. Family Hiking Club | Saturdays 8:00 AM-12:00 PM | All Ages | $40 per family
5. Teen Coding Bootcamp | Mon-Fri 1:00-3:00 PM | Ages 13-17 | $200
6. Toddler Music | Fridays 10:30-11:15 AM | Ages 2-4 | $50
Note: Registration closes June 15. Members get 20% off. Drop-in fee: $10 per session where space allows.

mcq_questions.question_text: The Wong family has two children, ages 7 and 11. They want one program both kids can attend together. Which is best?

Ground Floor: Entrance (south), Information Desk (centre), Children's Section (west wing), Computer Lab with 12 stations (east wing, near washrooms), Quiet Study Room A (north-east corner, reservable, max 4 people), Café (south-east corner, open 8 AM–4 PM). diagram.text_description:
Riverside Public Library — Floor Guide:
Ground Floor: Entrance (south), Information Desk (centre), Children's Section (west wing), Computer Lab — 12 stations (east wing, near washrooms), Quiet Study Room A (north-east corner, reservable, max 4 people), Café (south-east corner, open 8 AM–4 PM).
Upper Floor: Adult Fiction (west), Non-Fiction & Reference (east), Meeting Room B (north-west corner, holds 30, requires booking), Rooftop Reading Terrace (south side, open May–September, weather permitting).
Note: Free Wi-Fi throughout. Printing: $0.10/page B&W, $0.25/page colour. Library hours: Mon–Sat 9 AM–8 PM, Sun 12–5 PM.

Activities section: Live music stage (12 PM–4 PM, featuring local bands), International Food Court (20+ vendors, cash and debit accepted), Kids' Zone (face painting, bouncy castle, petting zoo — free for children under 10), Craft Market (handmade goods from local artisans), Cultural Dance Performances (1 PM, 2:30 PM, 4 PM on main stage). diagram.text_description:
Sunnydale Multicultural Fair — Saturday, June 8
Time: 11 AM–5 PM
Location: Sunnydale Community Park, 45 Lakeview Drive
Activities:
- Live Music Stage: 12 PM–4 PM (local bands)
- International Food Court: 20+ vendors (cash and debit accepted)
- Kids' Zone: face painting, bouncy castle, petting zoo — free for children under 10
- Craft Market: handmade goods from local artisans
- Cultural Dance Performances: 1 PM, 2:30 PM, 4 PM on main stage
Admission: Free
Parking: Free lot at Sunnydale Arena; overflow at St. Mark's Church ($5 donation suggested)
Rain plan: moves indoors to Sunnydale Community Centre gymnasium
Contact: info@sunnydalefair.ca

diagram.image_prompt: A transit system map titled 'Metro City Transit Authority — Route Map & Fare Guide'. Lines: Blue Line (Airport → Downtown Central → University → Riverside, runs 5 AM–12 AM, every 10 min peak / 20 min off-peak), Red Line (Northgate → City Hall → Downtown Central → Southport, runs 6 AM–11 PM, every 12 min peak / 25 min off-peak), Green Line (Eastview → Hospital District → Downtown Central, runs 5:30 AM–10:30 PM, every 15 min all day). Transfer station: Downtown Central (all lines). Fare zones: Zone 1 (core) $3.25 single, Zone 2 $4.50 single, Cross-zone $5.75. Day pass: $11.00. Monthly pass: $98 (all zones). Seniors/students: 35% discount on all fares. Children under 5: free. Note: 'Bikes allowed on all lines outside peak hours (7–9 AM, 4–6:30 PM). Accessibility: all stations wheelchair accessible. Lost & found: Downtown Central station office.' Use a classic transit map style with coloured lines and station dots.
diagram.text_description:
Metro City Transit Authority — Route Map & Fare Guide:
Blue Line: Airport → Downtown Central → University → Riverside | 5 AM–12 AM | Every 10 min peak / 20 min off-peak
Red Line: Northgate → City Hall → Downtown Central → Southport | 6 AM–11 PM | Every 12 min peak / 25 min off-peak
Green Line: Eastview → Hospital District → Downtown Central | 5:30 AM–10:30 PM | Every 15 min all day
Transfer station: Downtown Central (all lines connect)
Fares: Zone 1 $3.25 | Zone 2 $4.50 | Cross-zone $5.75 | Day pass $11.00 | Monthly pass $98 (all zones)
Seniors/Students: 35% discount on all fares. Children under 5: free.
Bikes: allowed on all lines outside peak hours (7–9 AM, 4–6:30 PM).
All stations wheelchair accessible. Lost & found at Downtown Central station office.

diagram.image_prompt: A hospital visitor information sheet titled 'Clearview Regional Hospital — Visitor Guide'. Sections: Visiting Hours — General Wards: 10 AM–12 PM and 4 PM–8 PM daily; ICU: 11 AM–12 PM and 6 PM–7 PM (max 2 visitors at a time); Maternity: 9 AM–9 PM (partners may stay overnight); Pediatrics: 8 AM–8 PM (one parent may stay overnight). diagram.text_description:
Clearview Regional Hospital — Visitor Guide:
Visiting Hours:
- General Wards: 10 AM–12 PM and 4 PM–8 PM daily
- ICU: 11 AM–12 PM and 6 PM–7 PM (max 2 visitors at a time)
- Maternity: 9 AM–9 PM (partners may stay overnight)
- Pediatrics: 8 AM–8 PM (one parent may stay overnight)
Parking: Main lot $4/hr, max $16/day. Validated parking at Info Desk: first 2 hours free.
Cafeteria: Ground floor, 7 AM–7 PM. Tim Hortons kiosk: main lobby, 6 AM–10 PM.
Gift Shop: Main lobby, 9 AM–5 PM weekdays only.
Rules: No flowers in ICU. Scent-free environment. Hand sanitizer before entering patient rooms. Masks required Nov–Mar (flu season).
Shuttle from Clearview GO Station every 30 min, 6 AM–10 PM.

Three lines: Lakeshore West (Oakville → Clarkson → Port Credit → Long Branch → Union Station, travel time 48 min end-to-end), Lakeshore East (Union Station → Danforth → Scarborough → Pickering → Oshawa, travel time 55 min), Midtown Crosslink (Pearson Airport → Weston → Bloor-Yonge → Danforth, travel time 32 min). Transfers: Union Station (Lakeshore West ↔ East), Danforth (Lakeshore East ↔ Midtown Crosslink). Service frequency: Peak (6:30–9:30 AM, 3:30–7 PM) every 15 min all lines; Off-peak every 30 min; Weekend every 30 min, no Midtown Crosslink on Sundays. Fares: Single trip $5.90 base + $1.40/zone crossed (max 4 zones). Day pass $14.50. Weekly pass $52. Monthly pass $172. Students/Seniors 40% off. Presto card users: 8% discount on single fares. Free transfers within 2 hours of tap. Bikes: front car only, max 2 per train. Design like a professional transit authority map.
diagram.text_description:
Lakeshore Regional Transit — Commuter Rail Network:
Lakeshore West: Oakville → Clarkson → Port Credit → Long Branch → Union Station (48 min)
Lakeshore East: Union Station → Danforth → Scarborough → Pickering → Oshawa (55 min)
Midtown Crosslink: Pearson Airport → Weston → Bloor-Yonge → Danforth (32 min)
Transfers: Union Station (West ↔ East), Danforth (East ↔ Midtown)
Peak (6:30–9:30 AM, 3:30–7 PM): every 15 min. Off-peak/Weekend: every 30 min. No Midtown Crosslink on Sundays.
Fares: $5.90 base + $1.40/zone (max 4 zones). Day pass $14.50. Weekly $52. Monthly $172.
Students/Seniors: 40% off. Presto: 8% off single fares. Free transfers within 2 hours.
Bikes: front car only, max 2 per train.

diagram.image_prompt: A workplace safety training requirements poster titled 'Safeway Distribution Centre — Mandatory Training Requirements'. Table format with columns: Training Module, Who Must Complete, Frequency, Duration, Where. Rows: (1) WHMIS 2015 — All employees — Annually — 2 hours — Online portal; (2) Forklift Certification — Warehouse staff only — Every 3 years — 8 hours (1-day course) — On-site with certified trainer; (3) Fire Safety & Evacuation — All employees — Annually — 1 hour — Lunch-and-learn (various dates); (4) First Aid & CPR — Team leads and supervisors — Every 2 years — 16 hours (2-day course) — St. John Ambulance off-site; (5) Ergonomics & Lifting — Warehouse and shipping staff — Once (at hire) + refresher annually — 45 minutes — On-site demonstration; (6) Workplace Violence Prevention — All employees — Every 2 years — 90 minutes — Online portal. Footer: 'Training records maintained by HR. Failure to complete mandatory training by deadline may result in temporary reassignment. Employees are paid for all training time. Questions? Contact safety@safeway-dc.ca.' Use a serious industrial safety design with yellow/black accents and safety icons.
diagram.text_description:
Safeway Distribution Centre — Mandatory Training Requirements:
1. WHMIS 2015 | All employees | Annually | 2 hours | Online portal
2. Forklift Certification | Warehouse staff only | Every 3 years | 8 hours (1 day) | On-site with trainer
3. Fire Safety & Evacuation | All employees | Annually | 1 hour | Lunch-and-learn
4. First Aid & CPR | Team leads and supervisors | Every 2 years | 16 hours (2 days) | Off-site (St. John Ambulance)
5. Ergonomics & Lifting | Warehouse and shipping staff | At hire + annual refresher | 45 min | On-site
6. Workplace Violence Prevention | All employees | Every 2 years | 90 min | Online portal
Note: Training records maintained by HR. Missed deadlines may result in temporary reassignment. All training time is paid. Contact: safety@safeway-dc.ca.

Buildings and locations: Main Building (M) — Registrar (M-101, Mon–Fri 9 AM–4:30 PM), Financial Aid (M-102, Mon–Fri 10 AM–3 PM, by appointment Tue/Thu after 3 PM), Academic Advising (M-200, drop-in Mon/Wed 1–4 PM, appointments other days). diagram.text_description:
Laurier University — Student Services Directory:
Main Building (M): Registrar M-101 Mon–Fri 9 AM–4:30 PM | Financial Aid M-102 Mon–Fri 10 AM–3 PM (appointments Tue/Thu after 3 PM) | Academic Advising M-200 drop-in Mon/Wed 1–4 PM, appointments other days

Health Centre (H): Walk-in Mon–Fri 9 AM–12 PM, appointments 1–4 PM; mental health counsellor Wed/Fri by referral

Career Centre (C): Resume reviews drop-in Tue/Thu 10 AM–2 PM; mock interviews by appointment; job board updated weekly
International Student Office (I): Visa support Mon–Fri 10 AM–4 PM; conversation circle Fri 3–4:30 PM
Note: Valid student ID required. Campus shuttle every 20 min between Main Building and Athletics, 7:30 AM–9 PM.

diagram.image_prompt: A provincial park trail map titled 'Silver Lake Provincial Park — Trail Guide'. Trails shown with coloured lines: Lakeshore Loop (blue, 3.2 km, easy, flat terrain, wheelchair accessible, estimated 45 min); Pine Ridge Trail (green, 5.8 km, moderate, some hills, estimated 1.5 hr); Summit Trail (red, 9.1 km, difficult, steep elevation gain 340m, estimated 3–4 hr, not recommended in wet conditions); Wetland Boardwalk (yellow, 1.5 km, easy, birding platform at midpoint, estimated 25 min). Facilities: Visitor Centre (open May–Oct 9 AM–5 PM daily, Nov–Apr weekends only 10 AM–3 PM), washrooms at trailheads and Visitor Centre, picnic area near Lakeshore Loop, canoe/kayak rental at beach ($20/hr, May–Sept). Day-use fee: $15/vehicle. Annual pass: $120. Dogs on leash on Lakeshore Loop and Wetland Boardwalk only. No dogs on Pine Ridge or Summit Trail (wildlife protection zone). No camping. Emergency contact posted at all trailheads. Nature-themed design with illustrations of trees, lake, and wildlife.
diagram.text_description:
Silver Lake Provincial Park — Trail Guide:
Lakeshore Loop: 3.2 km | Easy | Flat, wheelchair accessible | ~45 min
Pine Ridge Trail: 5.8 km | Moderate | Some hills | ~1.5 hr
Summit Trail: 9.1 km | Difficult | Steep, 340m elevation gain | ~3–4 hr | Not recommended in wet conditions
Wetland Boardwalk: 1.5 km | Easy | Birding platform at midpoint | ~25 min
Facilities: Visitor Centre May–Oct daily 9 AM–5 PM, Nov–Apr weekends 10 AM–3 PM. Washrooms at trailheads and Visitor Centre. Picnic area near Lakeshore Loop. Canoe/kayak rental at beach $20/hr May–Sept.
Day-use: $15/vehicle. Annual pass $120. Dogs on leash — Lakeshore Loop and Wetland Boardwalk only. No dogs on Pine Ridge or Summit (wildlife zone). No camping. Emergency contact at all trailheads.

diagram.image_prompt: A detailed municipal waste collection guide titled 'City of Maplewood — 2024 Waste & Recycling Collection Calendar'. Three-column layout: Stream, Collection Day, What Goes In. Rows: Green Bin (organics) — collected every week year-round (Zone A: Monday, Zone B: Tuesday) — food scraps, soiled paper, yard waste (max 4 bags yard waste per collection, must use paper bags or open-top bin); Blue Box (recycling) — collected every two weeks (Zone A: alternating Mondays starting Jan 8, Zone B: alternating Tuesdays starting Jan 9) — paper, cardboard, clean plastics #1–#5, glass bottles, metal cans (NO plastic bags, Styrofoam, or electronics); Garbage — collected every two weeks (opposite week from Blue Box) — max 3 bags/containers, each under 20 kg; Bulky Items — by appointment only, call 905-555-0300 or book online, $25 per pickup, max 5 items (mattresses, furniture, appliances — no hazardous materials); Hazardous Waste — depot drop-off only at 200 Industrial Rd, Sat 8 AM–4 PM (paint, batteries, chemicals, motor oil, electronics). Holiday schedule: No collection on statutory holidays — shifts to next business day. Missed collection? Report by 8 PM same day at maplewood.ca/waste or 905-555-0300. Use a clean municipal design with green/blue/grey icons for each stream.
diagram.text_description:
City of Maplewood — 2024 Waste & Recycling Collection Calendar:
Green Bin (organics): Weekly year-round. Zone A Monday, Zone B Tuesday. Food scraps, soiled paper, yard waste (max 4 bags, paper bags or open bin).
Blue Box (recycling): Every 2 weeks. Zone A alternating Mondays (from Jan 8), Zone B alternating Tuesdays (from Jan 9). Paper, cardboard, clean plastics #1–#5, glass, metal cans. NO plastic bags, Styrofoam, electronics.
Garbage: Every 2 weeks (opposite week from Blue Box). Max 3 bags/containers, each under 20 kg.
Bulky Items: By appointment — 905-555-0300 or online. $25/pickup, max 5 items. Mattresses, furniture, appliances. No hazardous materials.
Hazardous Waste: Depot only at 200 Industrial Rd, Sat 8 AM–4 PM. Paint, batteries, chemicals, motor oil, electronics.
Holiday: No collection on stat holidays — shifts to next business day.
Missed collection? Report by 8 PM same day at maplewood.ca/waste or 905-555-0300.

mcq_questions.question_text: Jana in Zone A had her Blue Box collected on January 8. When is her next Blue Box collection?

options.A: Put both out with regular garbage
options.B: Book a bulky item pickup for the AC; take paint to the hazardous waste depot
options.C: Put both in the Blue Box
options.D: Take both to the hazardous waste depot

mcq_questions.question_text: A Zone B resident puts out 5 bags of yard waste on a Tuesday. What is the problem?

mcq_questions.question_text: Monday is a statutory holiday. When will Zone A's Green Bin be collected?

options.A: It will not be collected that week

mcq_questions.question_text: Sarah puts a Styrofoam container and old batteries in her Blue Box. Which items will be accepted?

options.D: Neither — Styrofoam and electronics/batteries are excluded from Blue Box
